title: Print-saver layouts (max pieces per sheet) per family
context: Standard layouts prioritise visual clarity and ease of cutting, with generous spacing between pieces. For high-volume use -- a teacher printing 30 class sets, or a programme printing for an entire school -- paper and ink costs matter significantly. A print-saver layout packs the maximum number of pieces per sheet while maintaining the calibration bar and safe margins, reducing the number of pages required per set.
objective: Implement a print-saver layout mode for the generator engine that maximises pieces per sheet for each manipulative family while maintaining all style-guide requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: Print-saver layout mode is activated via a spec flag (e.g. 'layout: print-saver').
Maximises pieces per sheet using a bin-packing or strip-packing approach while respecting: safe margins, calibration bar placement, and minimum spacing between cut guides.
All standard variants (A4/Letter x colour/grayscale/ink-saver) are supported in print-saver mode.
Print-saver output passes the verification harness: dimensional correctness is unchanged (the packing only changes position, not size).
MIT-licensed; all commits DCO-signed; passes pnpm build, pnpm test, and CI gate.
resources: tasks/math-manip-code-001.json -- parametric generator engine (layout mode to extend)
tasks/math-manip-style-001.json -- style guide v1 (safe margins and calibration bar requirements)
output: A print-saver layout mode integrated into the generator engine, with tests and example outputs showing increased piece density, in a pull request, MIT-licensed.
